task_desc: Fix neuron-to-region classification mismatches between connectome dot panel and Brain3D/education panel, and add 3 orphaned neuron groups to 3D brain and education -- Two related data-consistency issues between the connectome dot panel (uses BRAIN.neuronRegions from connectome.js:100-128) and the Brain3D/education modules (brain3d.js REGION_DEFS, education.js EDUCATION_REGIONS): (1) Three neuron groups exist in the connectome, have weights, fire during simulation, and appear in neuronPopulations, but are completely absent from all Brain3D regions and education panel sections: NOCI (nociception/pain, connectome.js:107, fires on rapid touch per connectome.js:355), GNG_DESC (gnathal ganglia descending, connectome.js:114), and CLOCK_DN (circadian clock descending, connectome.js:116); the education panel intro says "59 functional neuron groups" but only describes 56 across its 10 region sections; add NOCI to the Mechanosensory region (it is a sensory pain pathway), GNG_DESC to the Subesophageal Zone region (gnathal ganglia is part of SEZ), and CLOCK_DN to the Central Complex or Drives region (circadian modulation), (2) Several neurons have conflicting type classifications causing visible color mismatches between the connectome dots (purple/central) and Brain3D/education (different colors): ANTENNAL_MECH is central in neuronRegions (connectome.js:114) but sensory in brain3d.js:104 and education.js:87; DN_WALK, DN_FLIGHT, DN_TURN, DN_BACKUP, DN_STARTLE, and VNC_CPG are central in neuronRegions (connectome.js:115-116) but motor in brain3d.js:85-86 and education.js:66; users viewing both panels simultaneously see contradictory color coding for the same neurons; update BRAIN.neuronRegions to match the brain3d/education categorization which is more neuroscientifically accurate (ANTENNAL_MECH is sensory input, DN_*/VNC_CPG are motor pathway). Files: js/connectome.js, js/brain3d.js, js/education.js.
